title: HTML SEO and Performance Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential tags and attributes for building fast and searchable web pages.

questions.question: How does a deep, complex DOM (many nested elements) affect a web page's performance?
options: It makes the page load faster by grouping data.
It can slow down rendering and increase memory usage.
It has no effect on performance.
It improves the ranking on search engines.

questions.question: Which <link> attribute is used to download critical resources (like fonts) early in the loading process?
options: rel='importance'
rel='preload'
rel='fetch'
rel='async'

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using 'loading=lazy' on an <img> tag?
options: It makes the image appear in black and white until clicked.
It improves initial load time by deferring image download until they are near the viewport.
It ensures the image is downloaded in the highest possible quality.
It automatically resizes the image for mobile screens.

questions.question: Which <link> attribute helps save time by establishing a connection to an external domain before a request is made?
options: rel='connect'
rel='preconnect'
rel='save'
rel='dns'

questions.question: Why should you use semantic tags like <h1> and <nav> for better SEO?
options: They automatically apply high-quality CSS styles.
They help search engine crawlers understand the hierarchy and meaning of your content.
They are required by the W3C to launch a website.
They encrypt the page content for better security.
